title: stylogyne cauliflora
intro: Stylogyne cauliflora, also known as the "false garlic" or " elephant garlic", is a plant species from the family Primulaceae. It is a small shrub native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia, known for its edible bulbs and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Stylogyne cauliflora can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine and natural health practices.
